_comment: All relative paths are resolved against the ai-tools dir. repoRoot is the cwd for both Claude agents and validation commands.
_commandsNote: Compilation and tests run inside Docker (gradle:jdk25 image) — no local JAVA_HOME/Gradle needed, all via Backend/docker-compose.test.yml's 'test' service. Compile steps use --no-deps (Postgres not needed just to compile). 'test' starts the test-db via depends_on and runs gradle test against it; the throwaway Postgres is kept WARM between iterations (no down -v) for speed. Tear it down after a run with: cd Backend && docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml -p career-coach-test down -v

commands.compileMain: cd Backend && HOST_UID=$(id -u) HOST_GID=$(id -g) docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml -p career-coach-test run --rm --no-deps -T test gradle --no-daemon -q --console=plain compileJava
commands.compileTests: cd Backend && HOST_UID=$(id -u) HOST_GID=$(id -g) docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml -p career-coach-test run --rm --no-deps -T test gradle --no-daemon -q --console=plain compileTestJava
commands.test: cd Backend && HOST_UID=$(id -u) HOST_GID=$(id -g) docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml -p career-coach-test run --rm -T test gradle --no-daemon --console=plain test

_stagingNote: Before pausing for human review the tool runs `git add -A -- <stagePaths>` so the agent's work is staged; any // TODO the human then adds shows up as an unstaged diff and is extracted on the next iterate.

claude._modelsNote: Opus writes the initial analysis and the final solution review; Sonnet handles interfaces, tests, implementation and routing.
